perturbation.original_text: This Franchise Agreement between Homewood Suites Franchise LLC ("we," "us," "our" or "Franchisor") and the Franchisee ("you," "your" or "Franchisee") set forth in the Addendum attached to this Agreement, is dated as of the Effective Date. We and you may collectively be referred to as the "Parties."
perturbation.changed_text: This Franchise Agreement between Homewood Suites Franchise LLC ("we," "us," "our" or "Homewood Suites") and the Franchisee ("you," "your" or "Hotel Operator") set forth in the Addendum attached to this Agreement, is dated as of the Effective Date. We and you may collectively be referred to as the "Signatories."
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Franchisor" to "Homewood Suites" and "Franchisee" to "Hotel Operator" and the 'Parties' to 'Signatories' introduces an in-text contradiction, as later sections rely on the initial definitions. This creates uncertainty about which entity holds the responsibilities and rights outlined in subsequent clauses. The contradictory aspect specifically arises because Homewood Suites is initially defined as the Franchisor but the document continues to use Franchisee throughout the document, assuming the Hotel Operator can be the same. There is no assurance that Hotel Operator and Franchisee means the same.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.0 GRANT OF LICENSE 2.1 Non-Exclusive License. We grant to you and you accept a limited, non-exclusive License to use the Marks and the System during the Term at, and in connection with, the operation of the Hotel in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. 6 2,2 Reserved Rights. 2.2.1 This Agreement does not limit our right, or the right of the Entities, to own, license or operate any Other Business of any nature, whether in the lodging or hospitality industry or not, and whether under the Brand, a Competing Brand, or otherwise. We and the Entities have the right to engage in any Other Businesses, even if they compete with the Hotel, the System, or the Brand, and whether we or the Entities start those businesses, or purchase, merge with, acquire, are acquired by, come under common ownership with, or associate with, such Other Businesses. 2.2.2 We may also 2.2.2.1 add, alter, delete or otherwise modify elements of the System; 2.2.2.2 use or license to others all or part of the System; 2.2.2.3 use the facilities, programs, services and/or personnel used in connection with the System in Other Businesses; and 2.2.2.4 use the System, the Brand and the Marks in the Other Businesses. 2.2.3 You acknowledge and agree that you have no rights to, and will not make any claims or demands for, damages or other relief arising from or related to any of the foregoing activities, and you acknowledge and agree that such activities will not give rise to any liability on our part, including liability for claims for unfair competition, breach of contract, breach of any applicable implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, or divided loyalty.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Franchisor' with 'Homewood Suites' and 'Franchisee' with 'Hotel Operator.' The contradicted text relies heavily on the terms 'Franchisee' and 'we' (defined as Franchisor). By changing these terms and the reference to 'Parties' to 'Signatories', the later use of 'Franchisee' in Section 2.0 GRANT OF LICENSE introduces ambiguity and creates a contradiction. The license is granted to 'you' which is now defined as 'Hotel Operator', but the rest of the agreement continues to use the term 'Franchisee' assuming they are the same. This misaligned terminology creates a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: "Gross Rooms Revenue" means all revenues derived from the sale or rental of Guest Rooms (both transient and permanent) of the Hotel, including revenue derived from the redemption of points or rewards under the loyalty programs in which the Hotel participates, amounts attributable to breakfast (where the guest room rate includes breakfast), and guaranteed no-show revenue and credit transactions, whether or not collected, at the actual rates charged, less allowances for any Guest Room rebates and overcharges, and will not include taxes collected directly from patrons or guests.
perturbation.changed_text: "Gross Rooms Revenue" means only the revenues derived from transient Guest Room rentals of the Hotel. Any revenue derived from loyalty programs or packages is explicitly excluded from this definition, as are taxes, fees and other assessments regardless of whether those are collected from patrons or guests.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the definition of "Gross Rooms Revenue" to exclude revenue from loyalty programs, breakfast packages, and taxes directly contradicts the original expansive definition. This introduces ambiguity and affects how the Monthly Fees are calculated, as subsequent sections rely on the original definition to calculate payments. Because it removes inclusions, this can make financial expectations for the franchisor unrealistic.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.2 Calculation and Payment of Fees. 8.2.1 The Monthly Fees will be calculated in accordance with the accounting methods of the then-current Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry, or such other accounting methods specified by us in the Manual. 8.2.2 The Monthly Fees will be paid to us at the place and in the manner we designate on or before the fifteenth (15th) day of each month and will be accompanied by our standard schedule setting forth in reasonable detail the computation of the Monthly Fees for such month. 8.2.3 We may require you to transmit the Monthly Fees and all other payments required under this Agreement by wire transfer or other form of electronic funds transfer and to provide the standard schedule in electronic form. You must bear all costs of wire transfer or other form of electronic funds transfer or other electronic payment and reporting. 8.2.4 In the event of fire or other insured casualty that results in a reduction of Gross Rooms Revenue, you will determine and pay us, from the proceeds of any business interruption or other insurance applicable to loss of revenues, an amount equal to the forecasted Monthly Fees, based on the Gross Rooms Revenue amounts agreed on between you and your insurance company that would have been paid to us in the absence of such casualty.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of "Gross Rooms Revenue," explicitly excluding revenue streams (loyalty programs, packages, taxes) that were previously included in the original definition. This directly contradicts section 8.2.4, which calculates Monthly Fees based on "Gross Rooms Revenue". The modified definition would result in a lower Gross Rooms Revenue figure, thus impacting the calculation of Monthly Fees during insured casualty events, creating a direct financial contradiction. Because it removes inclusions, this can make financial expectations for the franchisor unrealistic, as mentioned in the explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 13.2 Your Transfer. You understand and acknowledge that the rights and duties in this Agreement are personal to you and that we are entering into this Agreement in reliance on your business skill, financial capacity, and the personal character of you, your officers, directors, partners, members, shareholders or trustees. A Transfer by you of any Equity Interest, or this Agreement, or any of your rights or obligations under this Agreement, or a Transfer by an Equity Owner is prohibited other than as expressly permitted herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.2 Your Transfer. You understand and acknowledge that the rights and duties in this Agreement are NOT personal to you. A Transfer by you of any Equity Interest, or this Agreement, or any of your rights or obligations under this Agreement, or a Transfer by an Equity Owner is encouraged.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the personal nature of the contract and encouraging transfer of Equity Interest or the Agreement directly conflicts with the detailed restrictions on transfers outlined in the subsequent sub-sections of Section 13. This creates uncertainty and the modified wording gives a permission, to which the text later restricts. This will cause problems in the enforcement, or in case of litigation when invoking this term. Specifically, the changes mean that subsection 13.2.1 Permitted Transfers That Do Not Require Notice or Consent will be a direct contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.2.1 Permitted Transfers That Do Not Require Notice or Consent. The following Transfers are permitted without giving notice or obtaining our consent if the Permitted Transfer does not result in a change in Control of the Franchisee, the Hotel or the Hotel Site and you meet the requirements set forth below. 13.2.1.1 Privately Held Equity Interests: Less than 25% Change/No Change of Control. An Equity Interest that is not publicly traded may be Transferred if, immediately after the transaction, the transferee Equity Owner will own less than twenty-five percent (25%) of the Equity Interest. 13.2.1.2 Publicly Traded Equity Interests. A Publicly Traded Equity interest may be Transferred.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 13.2 states that the rights and duties are NOT personal and encourages the transfer of Equity Interest. This directly contradicts Section 13.2.1, which outlines specific conditions and limitations on permitted transfers, some of which don't require notice or consent. By encouraging all transfers in 13.2, the detailed stipulations in 13.2.1 become contradictory, creating uncertainty about which rules govern transfers. The encouraged transfer conflicts with the permitted transfer that doesn't require notice or consent because it is no longer regulated according to the specific cases.
